title: A new radical left is shaking up the Democratic party
subhead: A new generation of candidates, critical of elites, social injustice and US support for Israel, is making its mark in the Democratic primaries ahead of November's midterm elections.

Nearly 35,000 people became millionaires in France in 2025, UBS study finds
subhead: The number of millionaires worldwide has reached record highs, according to the report. Real estate and finance remain the most effective ways to build wealth.

title: Iran and US negotiations stall over future of Strait of Hormuz
subhead: Despite assurances from Washington, negotiations over the Strait of Hormuz have not been smooth sailing. The possibility of an Iranian and Omani toll continues to divide policymakers, with some seeing it as the least worst option.

title: Bad Bunny brings Puerto Rican pride to Marseille
subhead: Performing at the Stade-Vélodrome on Wednesday, the superstar switched between salsa, plena and reggaeton. He is set to perform in Paris on Saturday and Sunday.

title: How can we prepare together for the next heatwave?
subhead: France will experience many more heatwaves and some will be even more severe than the last one, which was historic. To cope, we need to get organized collectively – without giving up the fight to reduce our greenhouse gas emissions, Le Monde's climate expert writes.

250 years of American independence: In 1976, a bicentennial celebrated with patriotism despite divisions
subhead: 'America 250' (10/13). In 1976, the United States turned a page on one of the most turbulent chapters in its history. After the Vietnam War and the resignation of Richard Nixon, the bicentennial offered Americans a moment of relief. Minority rights, social justice – new debates continued to stir society, notably led by the economist Jeremy Rifkin and the 'red, white and blue' left.

title: Monaco explosion: The Ukrainian-born oligarch's shady business dealings
subhead: Vadym Yermolaiev, a wealthy businessman, was targeted by a homemade explosive bomb on Monday. Despite renouncing his Ukrainian nationality, he continued business activities connected to his country of origin.
byline: Lucas Minisini,  Sofia Fischer and  Thomas d’Istria

title: 'We should not accept that heatwaves cause thousands of deaths. That is not normal'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Dossier, founder of the environmental consulting firm Quattrolibri, offers an initial political assessment of how France responded to the most intense heatwave ever recorded in the country.
